title: Future Drama

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, there's your girlfriend.
jokes.punchline: Mirror comeback about Bart's girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Predictable comeback, very standard sibling banter

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you love Moleman. No, you do. You're gay for Moleman! You're gay for Moleman!
jokes.setup: Bart escalates the teasing about romantic interests
jokes.punchline: The absurd repetition and specificity of 'gay for Moleman'
jokes.explanation: The specificity of Moleman and the repeated chanting creates a memorable absurd moment

jokes.text: No one's gay for Moleman.
jokes.setup: Bart's escalating chant about being gay for Moleman
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact dismissal of the entire premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that deflates Bart's energy while being oddly profound

jokes.text: Don't you mean astronomy? No, my dear, I said astrology.
jokes.setup: Lisa corrects what seems like a mistake between astronomy and astrology
jokes.punchline: Frink insists he meant the pseudoscience, not the real science
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intelligence and Frink's eccentric confidence in pseudoscience

jokes.text: For years, you see, astrology was the Tampa Bay Devil Rays of the sciences... not so good.
jokes.setup: Comparison between astrology and a notoriously bad baseball team
jokes.punchline: The understated 'not so good' after the elaborate comparison
jokes.explanation: Clever sports analogy with good timing on the understated punchline

jokes.text: Oh, it's so great. We can do anything now that scientists have invented magic.
jokes.punchline: The oxymoronic concept of 'scientists inventing magic'
jokes.explanation: Clever contradiction that captures both future wonder and scientific absurdity

jokes.text: Lisa, graduating two years early, and, Bart... graduating.
jokes.punchline: The low bar set for Bart - just graduating is an achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing different expectations for each child, economically delivered

jokes.text: Well, maybe I was partying so hard with my single friends last night, I forgot the rules.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to justify his behavior
jokes.punchline: The obviously false bravado about 'partying with single friends'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment - pathetic attempt at seeming cool

jokes.text: Could you please stop fighting in front of us? Why? Is it messing you up?
jokes.setup: Lisa asks parents to stop fighting in front of them
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual interest in whether it's actually harmful
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parental negligence delivered with Homer's characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: Stupid wildlife, serves 'em right.
jokes.setup: Homer's reaction to Maggie in Alaska
jokes.punchline: His inexplicable hostility toward wildlife
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur hostility toward random things

jokes.text: I've been turning him down for the prom since kindergarten.
jokes.setup: Lisa's disgust at Milhouse being her date
jokes.punchline: The absurd timeline of rejecting prom invitations since kindergarten
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Milhouse-Lisa dynamic with absurd persistence

jokes.text: You relented at age 12 when he pulled you out of a house fire. Which you later found out he started.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse caused the fire he 'rescued' her from
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark twist on romantic rescue trope, perfect Milhouse character moment

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late, Lisa. I was at the gym, totally gunning my lats. Just gunning them.
jokes.setup: Milhouse trying to sound tough and athletic
jokes.punchline: The awkward repetition of 'gunning' and obvious try-hard nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse trying too hard, the repetition shows his nervousness

jokes.text: The deposit on this tux was 200 Reagans.
jokes.punchline: Currency named after Reagan in the future
jokes.explanation: Clever worldbuilding detail about future currency

jokes.text: Bart, you're lookin' crook-ed. Hey, Jenda, how 'bout some forehead?
jokes.punchline: The bizarre physical greeting of head-butting
jokes.explanation: Decent future slang joke with physical comedy element

jokes.text: That was jagged. when I was a kid we didn't show our affection by head-butting.
jokes.punchline: Marge's old-fashioned reaction to future customs
jokes.explanation: Solid generational humor about changing customs

jokes.text: Who knew that bio-engineered food would lead to smart puke?
jokes.punchline: The concept of intelligent vomit as an unintended consequence
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: You think you're better than me, eh, puke?
jokes.punchline: Homer's confrontational attitude toward his own vomit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - getting defensive about his intellectual inferiority to his own vomit

jokes.text: So, Nelson, who did you end up bringing tonight? Sherri or Terri? Uh, it didn't seem fair to choose.
jokes.setup: Question about Nelson's prom date choice between twins
jokes.punchline: Nelson brought both, showing his characteristically aggressive solution
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nelson's direct approach to problems

jokes.text: Welcome, seniors and sexually precocious underclassmen.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate acknowledgment of younger students' sexuality
jokes.explanation: Mildly transgressive humor that fits Skinner's awkwardness

jokes.text: I'd like to say good-bye to those who are going off to college, or to fight in Gulf War Five: 'Operation Find Our President's Head.'
jokes.punchline: Absurd future war with ridiculous mission name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of endless wars and political absurdity

jokes.text: That means no booze, kick, puff, doze, maxx, stim or turb.
jokes.punchline: List of made-up future drug names
jokes.explanation: Solid worldbuilding humor with believable future drug names

jokes.text: Remember, 'Stim Kills.' It's what turned Superintendent Chalmers into a vegetable.
jokes.punchline: Chalmers became a literal vegetable from drugs
jokes.explanation: Dark fate for familiar character works as cautionary tale

jokes.text: Skinner. Skinner. Skinner.
jokes.punchline: He can only repeat Skinner's name, his obsession continues
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - even brain-damaged, he's still fixated on Skinner

jokes.text: I can use the potty now.
jokes.punchline: Childlike pride in basic bathroom function
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor about disability, somewhat cheap

jokes.text: Oh, sorry, Lisa. Sink activate. I love your dress. Soap activate.
jokes.punchline: Voice-activated bathroom fixtures interrupting conversation
jokes.explanation: Good future tech gag that creates natural interruption

jokes.text: Yeah, even though McDonald's owns Yale now, it's still a great school.
jokes.setup: Lisa talking about getting into Yale
jokes.punchline: Corporate ownership of prestigious universities
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate influence on education

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, that's the thing he had to do as punishment for stealing Christmas. Yeah. I miss Christmas. I don't.
jokes.setup: Burns had to give scholarships as punishment
jokes.punchline: He literally stole Christmas, and some don't miss it
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization and generational divide on Christmas

jokes.text: Travel. Do you need a traveling companion, perchance? Travel canceled.
jokes.setup: Milhouse eagerly offers to travel with Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately cancels his plans rather than go with Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character dynamics, Bart's instant rejection is brutal and funny

jokes.text: I can fly. I can fly. Just kidding, I know I can't fly. I can glide, though.
jokes.setup: Dangerous driver claiming he can fly
jokes.punchline: Clarification that he can only glide, still dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and false reassurance that's still alarming

jokes.text: Sex on prom night is as American as our 51st state: Saudi-Israelia.
jokes.setup: Jenda talking about prom night sex being American
jokes.punchline: Absurd future state combining Saudi Arabia and Israel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire combined with unexpected geography

jokes.text: That's really sweet... but marriage is a three-year commitment.
jokes.punchline: Marriage commitment is much shorter in the future
jokes.explanation: Good future society gag about changing relationship expectations

jokes.text: You can waitress while I learn to cut meat. We'll live in a trailer, but to avoid paying for parking, I'll drive while you sleep. We'll never, ever stop.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to convince Jenda to marry him
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awful life plan ending in perpetual motion
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating horror of Bart's life plan, each detail worse than the last

jokes.text: When? Just this minute.
jokes.setup: Bart asks when she outgrew him
jokes.punchline: She literally just realized it during his speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - his speech was so bad it changed her feelings instantly

jokes.text: Here, you want some chicken? Just grab it with your hands, go ahead. We're not fancy here.
jokes.punchline: His social awkwardness and defensive attitude about not being fancy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Comic Book Guy's social inadequacy

jokes.text: Son, sometimes people just grow apart, like me and your mom. No, Mom dumped you 'cause you blew all our money on this underwater house.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to give relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutal honesty about why the marriage failed and the absurd underwater house
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamics with Bart's directness and Homer's poor decisions

jokes.text: She'll realize she was a fool, when... Hey! Come back with my patio furniture!
jokes.punchline: Immediate evidence of the house's problems as fish steal furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - his defense is immediately undermined by reality

jokes.text: Stupid flounders.
jokes.punchline: Homer's misdirected anger at the wrong type of fish
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misdirected hostility with wrong fish species

jokes.text: Why'd you buy the first hover car ever made? Didn't you know it'd take time to work out the kinks? I know! It's a hover car!
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike excitement overrode any practical considerations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - excitement trumps practicality every time

jokes.text: All right! You guys are my new best friends. You wish, loser!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make friends with strangers
jokes.punchline: Immediate harsh rejection
jokes.explanation: Standard rejection humor, works for Homer's pathetic situation

jokes.text: Celebrity Buttocks, Nipple Relocations and Fluffy Tails
jokes.punchline: The increasingly bizarre cosmetic procedures
jokes.explanation: Good background gag escalation, amusing future surgery trends

jokes.text: You came from my back fat!
jokes.punchline: The disgusting origin of the clone material
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with perfect economy - explains cloning source disgustingly

jokes.text: You moron! Why did I ever think I needed a clone? Hey, I'm not the clone, you're the clone! Oh, please, not this again.
jokes.punchline: Identity confusion and the implication this argument happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Good setup for ongoing clone confusion as recurring problem

jokes.text: Want it to get weirder? I... don't think so.
jokes.setup: Edna offering to make the situation weirder
jokes.punchline: Bart's wise decision to avoid further discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good tension relief with Bart showing unexpected wisdom

jokes.text: I wish I could talk to my fourth-grade self just once. I'd say 'Work hard, don't be such a screw up.' This time is so precious. Don't waste it.
jokes.punchline: She's literally talking to her former fourth-grade student
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment - she's getting her wish without realizing it

jokes.text: Is that robot break-dancing? No. He activated his self-destruct mechanism.
jokes.setup: Bart misinterpreting robot movement as dancing
jokes.punchline: It's actually a suicide attempt
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good misdirection - dancing vs. self-destruction

jokes.text: What Lisa's problem? Milhouse mother say Milhouse handsome!
jokes.punchline: Regression to childlike speech and mommy validation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse breakdown - reverting to mama's boy when stressed

jokes.text: Armpit noises are not a language. Oh, yeah?
jokes.setup: Lisa dismissing Bart's claimed language skills
jokes.punchline: Bart's juvenile response proving her point
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart immaturity, predictable but in-character

jokes.text: He's from Springfield. I think you know him. Krusty the Clown is dating your mom!
jokes.setup: Marge introducing her new boyfriend mysteriously
jokes.punchline: It's the inappropriate choice of Krusty the Clown
jokes.explanation: Good setup and reveal, Krusty is an appropriately weird choice

jokes.text: Yes, Mr. Gheet. I'm Anoop, you racist cracker!
jokes.setup: Boss calling employee by wrong name
jokes.punchline: Employee correcting him while calling out racism
jokes.explanation: Good workplace racism commentary with direct confrontation

jokes.text: Which one? Radioactive, smallpox, eternal midnight... No, no. The one with the uni-clams.
jokes.punchline: The relatively benign-sounding uni-clam zone among deadly options
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of dangerous zones with absurd final choice

jokes.text: Dude, this is totally not the time to call me. I don't know. Some kind of pasta. Look, just get me what you're having, all right? Not that. What soups do they have...?
jokes.punchline: He's simultaneously ordering food during the crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - multitasking criminal with mundane priorities

jokes.text: My diamonds are going to be retro-morphed into coal... the most valuable substance on Earth.
jokes.punchline: Coal has become more valuable than diamonds in the future
jokes.explanation: Good future economics gag with resource value reversal

jokes.text: Once I home-invaded this Princeton dude, and he was like totally married.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to convince Bart that Ivy League is attractive
jokes.punchline: His evidence is from committing a home invasion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - casual admission of serious crime as social observation

jokes.text: Mr. Smithers? I thought you were... you know... No, I'm straight. As long as I take these injections every ten minutes.
jokes.punchline: Smithers needs medication to maintain heterosexuality
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on sexuality and medication with absurd premise

jokes.text: I love boobies!
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic and juvenile declaration
jokes.explanation: Overcompensating Smithers works for character but somewhat crude

jokes.text: It's so great that Yale has finally forbidden men from taking science. Now let's see, should I major in Femistry or Galgebra?
jokes.setup: Lisa at Yale discussing academic options
jokes.punchline: Gender-swapped science names in feminist future
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on academic subjects with social commentary

jokes.text: I mean, Hot Dog on a Stick Management Camp.
jokes.punchline: The dramatic downgrade to fast food management
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with specific absurd alternative, decent structure

jokes.text: Why don't you mail it to your butt?
jokes.setup: Skinner calling Bart to get his diploma
jokes.punchline: Bart's juvenile response at graduation
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but in-character for Bart, predictable humor

jokes.text: Bart, now that you've graduated I can finally say this: you really press my cider.
jokes.setup: Skinner finally able to speak freely to graduated Bart
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic insult that maintains his character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - finally honest but still euphemistic and awkward

jokes.text: Listen, girls... I'm going out for a pack of cigarettes. Isn't that what your father said the day he left you? Yeah. I never understood why he did it... till now. I'm coming, Papa!
jokes.punchline: Suddenly understanding his father's motivations and embracing the pattern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - cycle of abandonment with moment of understanding and acceptance

jokes.text: I'm not the first guy to get a nosebleed during a slow dance.
jokes.setup: Milhouse defending himself after Lisa broke up with him
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing reason for the breakup
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse embarrassing moment, very specific and pathetic

jokes.text: Is it because of my small calves? They're the hardest place to add mass!
jokes.setup: Milhouse wondering why Lisa dumped him
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific bodybuilding insecurity
jokes.explanation: Good specific insecurity that shows his gym obsession from earlier

jokes.text: And now you're an emotionally crippled mini-Hulk.
jokes.punchline: Perfect description of muscular but emotionally damaged Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Excellent character description that captures both physical and emotional state

jokes.text: Time to strike while the iron is sad!
jokes.punchline: Malapropism of 'strike while the iron is hot' that accidentally fits perfectly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism that works better than the original phrase for the situation

jokes.text: I never had this problem with Todd Flanders.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to relationship with innocent Todd
jokes.explanation: Mildly shocking reference that implies Jenda's dating history

jokes.text: Forget it. I had to sell all my marrow to pay the light bill.
jokes.punchline: Bart's extreme poverty requiring selling body parts
jokes.explanation: Dark economic humor about extreme poverty measures

jokes.text: I didn't have much marrow.
jokes.setup: Bart's admission of selling his bone marrow
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission that he didn't have much to sell anyway
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that makes the situation even more pathetic

jokes.text: Have you eaten onions lately? No. And it's really hard 'cause I'm on an all-onion diet.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about onions before kissing
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's absurd dietary restriction creating internal conflict
jokes.explanation: Great absurd premise that creates genuine romantic conflict

jokes.text: Sorry, we're ghosts now. Our spirits live in those two trees. Or, maybe we're alive and we just feel like jerkin' you around. Or maybe we're one of each.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for help from Lenny and Carl
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about their existence status with multiple possibilities
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about ambiguous character states and audience uncertainty

jokes.text: And you will. At the age of 83. You die one minute later. And my brain is put in a... Pauper's grave.
jokes.setup: Frink predicting Bart will find love
jokes.punchline: Immediate death and ignoble end for his brain
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - positive prediction immediately undercut by grim details

jokes.text: You want me to attend the funeral of the Sultan of Brunei? Well, I would consider it my honor. Hey, Brandine, pack my evenin' britches. We's goin' to Brunei.
jokes.setup: Vice President Cletus getting diplomatic request
jokes.punchline: His hillbilly response to formal international duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Cletus treating international diplomacy like a social visit

jokes.text: You want me to attend the funeral of the Sultan of Brunei? Well, I would consider it my honor. Hey, Brandine, pack my evenin' britches. We's goin' to Brunei.
jokes.setup: Vice President Cletus receiving diplomatic request
jokes.punchline: His folksy response to international diplomacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus character voice in absurd political situation, excellent contrast

tmdb_overview: Through Professor Frink's future machine, Bart and Lisa see their lives in the year 2013, where Bart steals Lisa's chances at going to an Ivy League school in order to impress a skater girl named Jenda while Homer (now separated from Marge and living in an underwater apartment) fights Krusty the Clown after Marge begins dating him.
